message:
fix(fft): match NumPy Inf-component propagation in FFT (franken_numpy-1xwh)

The FFT twiddle factors cos(k*π/2) produce near-zero values like 6e-17
instead of exact 0.0 due to floating point. When multiplied with Inf,
this produced NaN (0*Inf=NaN in IEEE 754), poisoning the entire output.

NumPy's pocketfft handles this correctly, producing results like
[-nan+0j, -2-infj, nan+0j, -2+infj] for input [1, inf, 3, -inf].

Fix: Add fft_mul() helper that treats near-zero values (|x| < 1e-14)
as exactly zero when the other operand is non-finite. This preserves
the mathematical intent of twiddle factors at multiples of π/2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
